message:
feat(storage,db): harden storage pipeline — WBQ scaling, commit coalescer sharding, atomic writes, path traversal fixes

Storage (mcp-agent-mail-storage):
- Increase WBQ channel capacity 256→8192 and drain batch cap 64→256
- Add WBQ enqueue timeout (100ms) with backpressure metrics
- Track enqueue-to-drain latency via Instant-based timestamps (not wall-clock)
- Shard commit coalescer across N workers with per-shard channels
- Add spill-to-disk overflow when coalescer channel is full (CoalescerSpillRepo)
- Implement commit_all() for spill recovery (git add --all + commit)
- Add coalescer_commit_all_with_retry with exponential backoff + lock cleanup
- Replace write_text/write_json with atomic_write_bytes (temp-then-rename)
- Harden resolve_archive_relative_path against traversal via non-existent
  intermediate dirs (manual component normalization fallback)
- Add resolve_source_attachment_path with base_dir sandboxing and config-gated
  absolute path allowance
- Fix update_thread_digest TOCTOU race with create_new + AlreadyExists fallback
- Expose wbq_flush() and flush_async_commits() public APIs
- Report WBQ and commit coalescer metrics (depth, capacity, peak, latency
  percentiles, over-80% duration, error counts) in global metrics snapshot

DB (mcp-agent-mail-db):
- Cap IN-clause parameter counts at 500 (MAX_IN_CLAUSE_ITEMS) to stay within
  SQLite SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER limits
- Replace timestamp panic on extreme values with Unix epoch fallback
- Add tests for extreme timestamp values and path traversal edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
